info.title: SecureAuth MFA OTP & CAPTCHA API
info.description:
Complete API documentation for the SecureAuth application.

## Running modes

### Monolith mode (`npm run start`)
- Single server on `:3000` implements registration (OTP → CAPTCHA → TOTP setup) and login (password → TOTP).

### Multi-server mode (`npm run start:multi`)
- **OTP/UI** server on `:3000`
- **CAPTCHA** server on `:5000`
- **MFA** server on `:8000`

In multi-server mode, login is **password → login-OTP → CAPTCHA → MFA**.

## Flows

### Registration (monolith & multi-server OTP server)
1. `POST /api/register` — Register & send OTP (email or SMS)
2. `POST /api/verify-email` — Verify the 6-digit OTP
3. `GET /api/captcha` — Get SVG CAPTCHA challenge (registration)
4. `POST /api/verify-captcha` — Verify CAPTCHA & get TOTP QR code
5. Scan QR code in Google Authenticator

### Login (multi-server)
1. `POST /api/login` — Password check, then send login OTP
2. `POST /api/verify-login-otp` — Verify login OTP
3. `GET /api/captcha` — Get SVG CAPTCHA challenge (login CAPTCHA server)
4. `POST /api/verify-captcha` — Verify CAPTCHA (login CAPTCHA server)
5. `POST /api/verify-mfa` — Verify TOTP (MFA server)

### Session
- `GET /api/me` — Get current logged-in user
- `POST /api/logout` — Destroy session

otpPort.description: Set this to your OTP/UI port (e.g. 3500).

captchaPort.description: Set this to your CAPTCHA port (e.g. 5500).

mfaPort.description: Set this to your MFA port (e.g. 8500).

tags.name: Registration
tags.description: Multi-step user registration with OTP, CAPTCHA, and TOTP setup

tags.name: Dev
tags.description: Development-only helpers (disabled unless enabled by env flags)

tags.name: Session
tags.description: Session management — get current user & logout

post.description:
Register a new user and send a 6-digit OTP to their email address or phone number via SMS.

**DEV MODE:** If `EMAIL_USER`/`EMAIL_PASS` are not set in `.env`, the OTP is logged to the server console instead of being emailed. Similarly for Twilio SMS.

example.error: Failed to send email after multiple attempts. Check configuration.

post.description: Verify the 6-digit OTP code that was sent during registration. On success, the next step is to complete the CAPTCHA challenge.

example.message: OTP verified. Please complete the CAPTCHA.

get.description:
Generate and return a new SVG CAPTCHA image.

- On `http://localhost:3000` this is the **registration CAPTCHA**.
- On `http://localhost:5000` this is the **login CAPTCHA** (requires login-OTP step first).

The CAPTCHA text is stored in the server session. Each call generates a new CAPTCHA (the old one is replaced).

**CAPTCHA Config:** 5 characters, 3 noise lines, colored.

401.description: Login CAPTCHA prerequisites not satisfied (multi-server CAPTCHA service only)

post.description:
Verify a CAPTCHA answer.

- On `http://localhost:3000` this completes **registration** and returns a TOTP QR code.
- On `http://localhost:5000` this completes the **login CAPTCHA step** and returns the next URL (MFA).

**Prerequisites:**
- CAPTCHA must have been fetched via `GET /api/captcha`
- For registration (OTP/UI server): OTP must already be verified
- For login (CAPTCHA service): login OTP must already be verified

value.message: CAPTCHA verified! Please scan the QR code to setup your Authenticator app.

400.description: OTP not verified or incorrect CAPTCHA

post.description:
Authenticate a registered user with their email/phone and password.

- In **monolith mode**, this returns `requireMfa: true`.
- In **multi-server mode**, this returns `requireLoginOtp: true` and sends a login OTP, then you must call `POST /api/verify-login-otp` before going to the CAPTCHA service.

200.description: Credentials valid — next step required (depends on mode)

example.message: Password valid. Please enter the OTP sent to your email/phone.

post.description:
Verify the login OTP after `POST /api/login`.

On success, the next step is to call the CAPTCHA service (`GET http://localhost:5000/api/captcha`).

400.description: No pending login or invalid OTP

post.description:
Verify the 6-digit TOTP code from Google Authenticator (or any TOTP-compatible app).

- Monolith mode: requires `identifier` and verifies it against the user's stored secret.
- Multi-server mode: verifies the code for the *pending login user* stored in session, and requires previous steps (login OTP + CAPTCHA) to be completed.

**Note:** TOTP codes rotate every 30 seconds.

200.description: MFA verified — user is logged in

post.description:
Creates a user with a generated TOTP secret + QR code without running the full registration flow.

Enabled only when `ALLOW_DEV_ENDPOINTS=true`.

400.description: Invalid request or user already exists

get.description: Health endpoint for CAPTCHA (`:5000`) and MFA (`:8000`) services.
Returns the profile of the currently logged-in user. Requires an active session (session cookie).

post.description: Destroys the current server-side session, effectively logging the user out. The user must login again after this.

phone.description: Phone number with country code (required when method = 'phone')

identifier.description: The email or phone used as the user identifier
Email or phone used during registration

message.example: OTP verified. Please complete the CAPTCHA.

captchaSvg.description: SVG markup of the CAPTCHA image

identifier.description: Email or phone used during registration (required for registration flow on `:3000`). In multi-server login CAPTCHA flow (`:5000`), identifier is taken from session.

message.example: CAPTCHA verified! Please scan the QR code to setup your Authenticator app.

qrCodeUrl.description: Base64-encoded QR code image for Google Authenticator

secret.description: TOTP secret for manual entry in Authenticator app

requireMfa.description: Monolith mode: indicates MFA verification is required

requireLoginOtp.description: Multi-server mode: indicates login-OTP verification is required next

message.example: Password valid. Please enter the OTP sent to your email/phone.

identifier.description: Monolith mode only: registered email or phone. Multi-server mode uses the pending login identifier from session.
